purpose: Execute a phased, verifiable hardening plan to achieve deterministic state synchronization and reliable publish opportunity capture.
design_intent: Use the existing file-based architecture, strengthen ordering and API authority guarantees, and isolate scheduler adaptation signals from recheck noise without relaxing fail-closed protections.
change_impact: Introduces API metadata and concurrency controls, frontend sync discipline changes, scheduler adaptation signal refactor, and additional test/runbook obligations.
problem_statement: Recent incidents showed that persistence, scheduler adaptation, and frontend polling can drift enough to reduce publish opportunity capture despite healthy gap margins.
goals: Enforce one authoritative control-state write path with deterministic error handling
Guarantee server-authoritative state convergence across mutation, polling, and restart boundaries
Decouple adaptive scheduler signal quality from short-interval gap recheck behavior
Make state provenance and decision rationale auditable for operators
non_goals: Migrating to a database-backed control store
Full UI redesign or routing overhaul
Changing publisher success definition away from redirect verification

phases.name: Persistence Foundations
scope: Use atomic temp-write + rename commit path
Fail control-panel API requests on persistence failure
Rollback runtime-only toggles on failed persist
exit_criteria: No fire-and-forget control persistence paths remain in control-panel flow
Integration tests pass with persistence-failure expectations

phases.name: State Version and Concurrency
scope: Add stateVersion and persistedAt to control APIs
Reject stale updates using expectedVersion precondition
Persist and expose mutation provenance metadata
exit_criteria: Conflicting writes return deterministic conflict status
All mutation responses include authoritative version metadata

phases.name: Frontend Authoritative Sync
scope: Adopt invalidate-and-replace state updates after mutations
Discard stale poll results using request ordering/version guards
Constrain dirty-form protection to editable fields only
deliverables: UI behavior tests for multi-request ordering
exit_criteria: No mutation path relies on optimistic local merge for correctness
Out-of-order responses cannot regress rendered control state

phases.name: Scheduler Signal Isolation
scope: Separate gap-policy recheck cadence from adaptation signal window
Backtest interval adaptation against recent history
deliverables: Scheduler tests covering recheck and adaptation interactions
exit_criteria: Adaptive interval remains stable under frequent gap-policy skips
Opportunity capture improves without violating gap policy

phases.name: Ops and Closeout
scope: Add operator-facing provenance visibility where missing
Close verification budget and archive debt
deliverables: Runbook updates for persistence and scheduler diagnostics
Final acceptance checklist mapped to success criteria
Snapshot and archive updates per execution loop
exit_criteria: All success criteria in state-authority-final-goal-v1 are met
No open archive debt for this plan

risks.name: Contract drift during API metadata rollout
risks.mitigation: Version API response fields and update reviewer-pack specs in same slice

risks.name: Behavior regression in scheduler cadence
risks.mitigation: Backtest with publisher history replay and targeted integration tests

risks.name: UI stale-state regressions in dirty edit mode
risks.mitigation: Automated stale-response tests plus manual multi-tab verification
